Electricity Cost Calculator

Calculate how much an appliance costs to run. Enter wattage, daily usage hours, and your electricity rate.

FAQ

How is electricity cost calculated?

Cost = (Watts × Hours per day × Days) / 1000 × Rate per kWh. Divide watts by 1000 to get kilowatts, then multiply by hours and rate.

What is a kWh?

A kilowatt-hour (kWh) is the energy used by a 1000-watt appliance running for 1 hour. It's the standard billing unit for electricity.

How can I reduce electricity costs?

Use energy-efficient appliances, turn off unused devices, use LED bulbs, and run heavy appliances during off-peak hours.
